markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Create the handoff receipt format proving research was translated into the WP and no future model must open sidecar papers to understand scope. This MT belongs to ResearchSpecTranslation. Convert research, operator intent, and current authority into self-contained WP/spec-ready requirements.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Research papers and GitHub/codebase findings are context inputs only; translate every accepted idea into Handshake-native requirements before activation.
Activation must cite SPEC_CURRENT and the v02.192 indexed bundle as current authority, not the v02.182 monolith alone.
scope.expected_behavior: ResearchBundleHandoffReceipt leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ResearchBundleHandoffReceipt is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: AC1 (ResearchBundleHandoffReceipt specified for no-context continuation) -> the typed handoff receipt exists as .GOV/task_packets/WP-KERNEL-009-Project-Knowledge-Index-Loom-Rich-Editor-v1/refinement.json activation_manager.handoff_summary (schema-typed fields REFINEMENT_PATH, REFINEMENT_CHECK, ENRICHMENT_NEEDED, NEW_STUBS_CREATED_OR_UPDATED, NEW_FEATURES_OR_CAPABILITIES_DISCOVERED, MAJOR_TECH_UPGRADE_ADVICE, REVIEW_FOCUS, NEXT_ORCHESTRATOR_ACTION) inside the hsk.refinement_contract@1 schema; the translation proof it carries is grounded in stub research_basis (sources_checked -> patterns_found -> rejected_options -> selected_approach) and translated_memory_system_spec.source_of_truth_after_this_stub

No-sidecar-paper guarantee -> stub research_basis.normative_status=non_normative_context_only + translation_rule state that no future model must open papers to understand scope; the MemGraphRAG local PDF is recorded as having no extractable text layer (sources_checked relevant_pattern) and its accepted substance is fully restated in translated_memory_system_spec and .GOV/spec/master-spec-v02.192/spec-modules/02-system-architecture.md#2.3.13.11

AC2 (exact files, state tables, constraints, failure modes, verification evidence) -> exact files pinned in this MT scope.relevant_files and scope.constraints; state tables and APIs: stub architecture_seed.data_model_seed (native_research_translation_requirements) + core_components Native Research Translation Registry; failure modes: stub failure_scenarios (future model treats paper/GitHub as required authority); verification evidence: refinement.json refinement.verification_results (4x PASS, 1x BLOCKED_PRE_EXISTING)

AC3 (sidecar research rejected, ideas restated Handshake-native) -> same artifacts as AC1/AC2; every accepted research idea restated in WP fields and v02.192 spec sections; this MT scope.constraints bar sidecar-paper and outside-runtime proof

AC4 (HBR rows named with concrete reasons) -> HBR-INT-001, HBR-INT-004, HBR-MAN-001, HBR-SWARM-001 = NOT_APPLICABLE for runtime proof on this research/spec-record MT (no product surface, code, test, or runtime state changed by it); their requirement content is recorded for implementation MTs in .GOV/spec/master-spec-v02.192/spec-modules/12-end-of-file-appendices.md#12.7 (HBR applicability row), 10-product-surfaces.md#10.15.8 (UserManual duty) and #10.20 (backend-navigation/parallel-agent receipts); per packet.json acceptance_matrix.note HBR proof is carried per-MT and whole-WP HBR review is the external validator batch pass

concerns: Contract freshness skew: scope.relevant_files and the activation constraint pin the v02.191 bundle while SPEC_CURRENT and all evidence are v02.192 (contracts hydrated 07:20Z after the 06:45Z spec bump); validator should read the constraint intent (indexed bundle, not v02.182 monolith), but a hygiene pass updating contract pins to v02.192 would remove the ambiguity.
adversarial_review.refutation_attempts: Opened every cited artifact (refinement.json, spec-enrichment-proposal.json, stub contract, v02.192 spec modules 02/07/10/11/12, spec-changelog.jsonl, SPEC_CURRENT.md, packet.json); attacked criterion coverage AC1-AC4, citation validity, specificity, v02.192 freshness, and scope-artifact existence. Verified activation_manager.handoff_summary carries all eight typed fields, research_basis chain (sources_checked -> rejected_options -> translation_rule), the MemGraphRAG no-extractable-text-layer note verbatim in sources_checked, and translated_memory_system_spec.source_of_truth_after_this_stub.

wp_validator_review_v1.review_scope: Validated only MTs that were READY_FOR_VALIDATION in WP-KERNEL-009; no ACP used.
evidence_basis: Subagent and local WP Validator review of only the MTs that were READY_FOR_VALIDATION at session read.
Direct packet status/dependency map checked before mutation.

Targeted local cargo probes passed for MT-054, MT-056, and MT-063 hardening paths: relationship_id_resists_separator_injection_collision; db_blocks_retired_to_accepted_resurrection_via_raw_sql; scratch_schema_apply_rollback_reapply.
MT-049..MT-064 were independently confirmed by the returned subagent slice using real PostgreSQL-backed test paths.
wp_validator_review_v1.residual_uncertainty: Validation was scoped to MTs already READY_FOR_VALIDATION. Concurrent WIP/untracked files outside those MTs were not inspected.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
